observations: Large ground-mounted solar array visible — multiple rows of panels on fixed tilt frames across an open grass paddock.
This appears to be a utility-scale or large commercial ground-mount site, not a residential rooftop install.
Site survey phase: worker is assessing array layout, orientation, and general condition of existing or proposed install area.
Panels appear oriented north-facing (consistent with Southern Hemisphere best practice) on fixed-angle steel frames.
Significant cloud cover present — good opportunity to note shading impact on yield during survey.
No workers, PPE, or customers visible in frame.

installQualityFlags.message: Grass/vegetation under and around arrays appears long — vegetation management plan must be confirmed to prevent shading on lower panel rows and fire risk in dry months.
Frame support posts appear to be driven ground stakes — verify footings are rated for local wind region (AS 1170.2) and soil bearing capacity before finalising design.
No visible conduit or cable management between rows — confirm DC cable routing plan to prevent UV degradation and vermin damage at ground level.
Partial cloud cover visible — log irradiance conditions during survey and ensure shade analysis tool (e.g. SolarEdge Designer or PVsyst) accounts for local cloud frequency.

timeOnTaskNote: Site survey of a ground-mount array this size typically takes 45–90 minutes. Ensure row spacing, orientation bearing, and cable run paths are all measured and photographed before leaving the site.
nextSteps: Walk each row and photograph frame footings, cable routing, and any panel damage or soiling — document with geotagged photos for the design report.
Measure and confirm row-to-row spacing to validate inter-row shading calculations match the proposed tilt angle and local latitude.
Locate the inverter/combiner box and switchboard; trace the proposed AC cable path back to the grid connection point and measure the run length.

visualCallouts.label: Long grass — shading risk
visualCallouts.message: Vegetation under panels is long — flag this for the vegetation management plan to avoid lower-row shading and fire risk.

visualCallouts.label: Check footing spec
visualCallouts.message: Ground stake footings here — confirm wind and soil ratings match AS 1170.2 for this region.

visualCallouts.label: No visible conduit
visualCallouts.message: No cable management visible between rows — confirm DC cable routing and conduit plan for ground-level protection.

spokenCue.say: Check frame footings and photograph all cable runs before leaving.
